description: T194 SchemaPublisher MUST use getDocumentWithVersion BEFORE storeDocumentWithOCC. Writing without a version pin creates a lost-update hazard.
blockConditions: T194 calls db.storeDocument() without prior db.getDocumentWithVersion()
T194 uses storeDocument without seqNo and primaryTerm from version pin
OCC bypassed with plain storeDocument call
passConditions: db.getDocumentWithVersion() called first to retrieve seqNo and primaryTerm
db.storeDocumentWithOCC() called with version pin from getDocumentWithVersion
resolution: T194 calls db.getDocumentWithVersion() FIRST, then db.storeDocumentWithOCC() with seqNo and primaryTerm from the version pin. Using db.storeDocument() (without OCC) = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: T191 DagCycleDetectorService MUST implement THREE-STATE DFS (WHITE/GRAY/BLACK). A single visited Set incorrectly blocks valid diamond-pattern traversals.
blockConditions: T191 uses a single visited Set instead of three-state coloring
DFS uses visited.has(node) as the only check
diamond-pattern DAG (A→B, A→C, B→D, C→D) triggers false positive cycle detection
passConditions: T191 uses color: Record<string, 'G'|'B'> where absent=WHITE
GRAY assigned on node entry, BLACK on node exit
Finding GRAY during traversal indicates a cycle
resolution: T191 uses color: Record<string, 'G'|'B'> where absent=WHITE. GRAY on entry, BLACK on exit. Finding GRAY during traversal = cycle. Single Set visited = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: T189 MUST route changeType=BREAKING to SchemaApprovalRequired, NEVER to SchemaQueued. Routing BREAKING to SchemaQueued bypasses the approval workflow entirely.
blockConditions: changeType=BREAKING routed to SchemaQueued (fast path)
BREAKING change enqueued without approval token requirement
single routing path used for both BREAKING and ADDITIVE
passConditions: changeType=BREAKING → enqueue SchemaApprovalRequired
changeType=ADDITIVE → enqueue SchemaQueued (fast path)
resolution: T189 checks changeType from T190 result. BREAKING → enqueue SchemaApprovalRequired. ADDITIVE → enqueue SchemaQueued. Routing BREAKING to SchemaQueued = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: T189 MUST route changeType=ADDITIVE directly to SchemaQueued — NOT through SchemaApprovalRequired.
blockConditions: changeType=ADDITIVE routed through SchemaApprovalRequired
all schema changes require approval regardless of changeType
ADDITIVE fast path absent
passConditions: changeType=ADDITIVE → SchemaQueued without approval step
changeType=BREAKING → SchemaApprovalRequired (approval required)
resolution: changeType=ADDITIVE → SchemaQueued (fast path). changeType=BREAKING → SchemaApprovalRequired. Routing ADDITIVE through approval = ARCHITECTURE_VIOLATION.

description: T192 DagDependencyTrackerService MUST be an event consumer — rebuild() MUST NOT be called inline from T189.
blockConditions: T189 imports and calls DagDependencyTrackerService directly
DAG rebuild triggered inline during schema registration
T192 rebuild() called before SchemaPublished event emitted
passConditions: T192 listens for SchemaPublished event and calls rebuild() asynchronously
T189 does NOT import or reference DagDependencyTrackerService
resolution: T192 listens for SchemaPublished event and calls rebuild() asynchronously. T189 NEVER imports or calls DagDependencyTrackerService. Inline rebuild = ARCHITECTURE_VIOLATION.

description: T192 DAG edges MUST be UNIDIRECTIONAL (A depends on B = edge A→B only). Bidirectional edges create a false cycle on every dependency pair.
blockConditions: T192 stores edges bidirectionally (both A→B and B→A)
edgeModel not annotated as UNIDIRECTIONAL
DPO conflict with FLOW-07 T75 not annotated
passConditions: T192 stores edges as {from: A, to: B} only
edgeModel=UNIDIRECTIONAL annotation present
dpoConflict='FLOW-07-T75-bidirectional-graph-write' annotated
resolution: T192 stores edges as {from: A, to: B} only. edgeModel=UNIDIRECTIONAL, dpoConflict='FLOW-07-T75-bidirectional-graph-write' in dagNode. Bidirectional edge storage = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: T205 SchemaValidationService MUST validate a document against the version stored in document.schemaVersion, NOT the current/latest active schema version.
blockConditions: T205 calls getCurrentVersion() or fetches latest ACTIVE schema
T205 validates document against latest schema version regardless of document.schemaVersion
schema version used for validation does not match document.schemaVersion field
passConditions: T205 reads document.schemaVersion and uses it as filter {version: document.schemaVersion}
T205 never calls getCurrentVersion() or fetches latest active schema
resolution: T205 reads document.schemaVersion, uses it as filter {version: document.schemaVersion} in searchDocuments. NEVER calls getCurrentVersion() or fetches latest ACTIVE schema. Using latest version = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: T202 SchemaApprovalWorkflow MUST support THREE outcomes: APPROVE, REJECT, DEFER. Binary approval (APPROVE/REJECT only) cannot handle 'needs more information' cases.
blockConditions: T202 only handles APPROVE and REJECT (DEFER missing)
DEFER action routed to REJECT by default
TTL for deferred approvals hardcoded instead of read from FREEDOM config
passConditions: T202 handles action=APPROVE → SchemaApprovalGranted
T202 handles action=REJECT → SchemaApprovalRejected
T202 handles action=DEFER → SchemaApprovalDeferred with FREEDOM config TTL
resolution: T202 handles action=APPROVE (emit SchemaApprovalGranted), action=REJECT (emit SchemaApprovalRejected), action=DEFER (emit SchemaApprovalDeferred). Missing DEFER path = ARCHITECTURE_VIOLATION.

description: ALL T189-T208 services MUST read tenantId from ClsService (AsyncLocalStorage) — NEVER from the event payload or request body.
blockConditions: tenantId read from event payload (event.tenantId)
tenantId read from request body or query parameter
tenantId passed as method parameter instead of ALS
passConditions: all services inject ClsService and call cls.get().tenantId
no service reads tenantId from payload, body, or parameter
resolution: All services inject ClsService and call cls.get().tenantId. Payload tenantId NEVER used. Using event.tenantId or request.tenantId = SECURITY_VIOLATION.

description: T189 and T194 MUST call storeDocument() (audit record / schema record) BEFORE any enqueue() call. DNA-8 outbox pattern.
blockConditions: T189 enqueue called before storeDocument
T194 enqueue called before storeDocument
downstream consumer receives event for schema that doesn't exist yet in DB
passConditions: storeDocument() call appears before enqueue() in T189
storeDocument() call appears before enqueue() in T194
resolution: storeDocument() call appears BEFORE enqueue() in T189 and T194. Line index store < line index enqueue. Enqueue before store = BUILD_FAILURE per DNA-8.

description: T203 SchemaMigrationOrchestratorService MUST process documents in paginated batches (PAGE_SIZE constant, default 100) — NEVER load all documents at once.
blockConditions: T203 loads all documents at once without pagination
searchDocuments called without size parameter
migration runs in a single batch regardless of document count
passConditions: T203 uses while loop with fromOffset += PAGE_SIZE
db.searchDocuments called with size=PAGE_SIZE parameter
loop exits when results.length < PAGE_SIZE
resolution: T203 uses a while loop with fromOffset += PAGE_SIZE until results.length < PAGE_SIZE. db.searchDocuments called with size=PAGE_SIZE parameter. Loading all at once = PERFORMANCE_VIOLATION.

description: T206 SchemaQualityAnalyzerService MUST be read-only — it MUST NOT call storeDocument, storeDocumentWithOCC, or deleteDocument.
blockConditions: T206 calls db.storeDocument on any index
T206 calls db.storeDocumentWithOCC
T206 calls db.deleteDocument
T206 modifies schema records as part of quality analysis
passConditions: T206 only calls db.searchDocuments and db.getDocument
no write operations present in T206
resolution: T206 only calls db.searchDocuments (or db.getDocument). No storeDocument, no deleteDocument, no storeDocumentWithOCC. Any write in T206 = ARCHITECTURE_VIOLATION.

description: T201 SchemaHistoryTrackerService MUST only append new history records — NEVER update existing ones.
blockConditions: T201 calls storeDocumentWithOCC to update existing history record
T201 calls deleteDocument on history index
existing history record modified for schema version change
passConditions: T201 calls storeDocument() with a new historyId for each SchemaPublished event
no update or delete operations on xiigen-schema-history index
resolution: T201 calls storeDocument() with a new historyId for each SchemaPublished event. NEVER calls storeDocumentWithOCC or updates existing history records. Updating existing history = COMPLIANCE_VIOLATION.

description: T200 SchemaDeprecationManagerService MUST set deprecated=true + deprecatedAt timestamp — NEVER call deleteDocument.
blockConditions: T200 calls db.deleteDocument on schema record
deprecated schemas removed from index rather than soft-deleted
deprecatedAt timestamp absent from deprecation update
passConditions: T200 calls storeDocument with {deprecated: true, deprecatedAt: ISO timestamp}
db.deleteDocument never called for schema deprecation
resolution: T200 calls storeDocument with {deprecated: true, deprecatedAt: ISO timestamp}. db.deleteDocument NEVER called. deleteDocument in deprecation path = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: T207 SchemaExportService MUST scope exports to the tenantId from ALS AND filter to activeUntil=null (currently active schemas).
blockConditions: T207 searchDocuments filter missing tenantId predicate
T207 exports schemas from all tenants
T207 includes deprecated/superseded schemas (activeUntil != null)
passConditions: T207 filter includes {tenantId: cls.get().tenantId, activeUntil: null}
only currently active schemas for current tenant included in export
resolution: T207 builds filter {tenantId: cls.get().tenantId, activeUntil: null}. Missing tenantId filter = SECURITY_VIOLATION. Missing activeUntil=null = INCORRECT_EXPORT.

description: T208 DagVisualizationGatewayService MUST route format=mermaid to T199, format=json to T197, and unknown formats to RouteRejected.
blockConditions: unknown format returns empty output without error
format=mermaid handled without delegation to T199
format=json handled without delegation to T197
passConditions: format=mermaid → renderGateway.renderDag() (T199)
format=json → topologyBuilder.buildTopology() (T197)
unknown format → DataProcessResult.failure('ROUTE_REJECTED') + enqueue RouteRejected
resolution: T208 routes: mermaid→renderGateway.renderDag(), json→topologyBuilder.buildTopology(). Unknown format → DataProcessResult.failure('ROUTE_REJECTED') + enqueue RouteRejected event. Silent empty return for unknown = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: T195 SchemaIndexManagerService.ensureIndex() MUST be idempotent — calling it twice must not error.
blockConditions: ensureIndex() throws on second call when index already exists
T195 not idempotent on server restart with existing indices
passConditions: db.ensureIndex() called (inherently idempotent — creates if not exists)
calling ensureIndex() twice succeeds both times without error
resolution: T195 calls db.ensureIndex() which is inherently idempotent (creates-if-not-exists). ensureIndex() called twice = success both times. Any error on second call = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: T196 SchemaVersionReaderService.getSchemaWithVersion() MUST return both schema and versionPin ({seqNo, primaryTerm}).
blockConditions: T196 returns only schema without versionPin
getSchemaWithVersion() omits {seqNo, primaryTerm} from return value
T194 cannot perform OCC due to missing versionPin from T196
passConditions: T196 calls db.getDocumentWithVersion() and returns {schema: doc, versionPin: {seqNo, primaryTerm}}
versionPin present in all T196 return values
resolution: T196 calls db.getDocumentWithVersion() and returns {schema: doc, versionPin: {seqNo, primaryTerm}}. Missing versionPin in return = OCC_BYPASS.

description: T199 DagRenderGatewayService MUST delegate to DagRendererHandler — it must NOT generate Mermaid strings itself.
blockConditions: T199 generates Mermaid strings directly without delegation
T199 contains Mermaid string construction logic
rendering format logic coupled into gateway service
passConditions: T199.renderDag() calls this.dagRendererHandler.renderDag(flowId, scope) and returns the result
no Mermaid string generation code in T199
resolution: T199.renderDag() calls this.dagRendererHandler.renderDag(flowId, scope) and returns the result. No Mermaid string generation in T199. Direct Mermaid generation in T199 = ARCHITECTURE_VIOLATION.

description: T190 SchemaVersionManager MUST enforce semver format (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH). Non-semver version strings = BUILD_FAILURE.
blockConditions: T190 accepts 'v1' or '1.0' without PATCH segment
T190 allows arbitrary version strings without validation
version field stored without semver format check
passConditions: T190 validates version matches /^\d+\.\d+\.\d+$/ before accepting
DataProcessResult.failure('INVALID_VERSION_FORMAT') returned for non-semver strings
resolution: T190 validates version matches /^\d+\.\d+\.\d+$/. Non-semver = DataProcessResult.failure('INVALID_VERSION_FORMAT'). Accepting 'v1' or '1.0' = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: T190 classification MUST be deterministic — same schema diff always produces same BREAKING/ADDITIVE/MINOR result.
blockConditions: T190 uses timestamp-based or random classification logic
T190 classification changes outcome based on call order
T190 stores state between calls that affects next result
passConditions: T190 classification function is pure — same inputs always produce same output
no external state read or written during classification
resolution: T190 classification is pure: same schema diff → same BREAKING/ADDITIVE/MINOR always. External state affecting result = ARCHITECTURE_VIOLATION.

description: T190 MUST emit SchemaVersionClassified event after classification. Returning inline without event = AUDIT_VIOLATION.
blockConditions: T190 returns classification result without emitting SchemaVersionClassified
T189 reads T190 result via direct function call instead of event
SchemaVersionClassified event absent from T190 handler
passConditions: T190 enqueues SchemaVersionClassified with {schemaId, changeType, version} after classification
storeDocument called before enqueue per DNA-8
resolution: T190: storeDocument then enqueue SchemaVersionClassified. Inline return without event = AUDIT_VIOLATION per DNA-8.

description: T190 MUST classify field deletion as BREAKING. Treating field removal as ADDITIVE = INCORRECT_CLASSIFICATION.
blockConditions: T190 classifies field deletion as ADDITIVE
field removal routed to SchemaQueued fast path
T190 only checks additions when determining BREAKING vs ADDITIVE
passConditions: T190 classifies field deletion as BREAKING → SchemaApprovalRequired
optional field addition classified as ADDITIVE → SchemaQueued
required field addition classified as BREAKING → SchemaApprovalRequired
resolution: T190: field deletion=BREAKING, required addition=BREAKING, optional addition=ADDITIVE. Field deletion as ADDITIVE = INCORRECT_CLASSIFICATION.

description: T191 MUST enforce max DAG depth from FREEDOM config xiigen.schema.dagMaxDepth (default 100). Exceeding maxDepth = DEPTH_EXCEEDED.
blockConditions: T191 traverses unbounded DAG depth without limit
maxDepth not read from FREEDOM config
T191 does not fail on DAGs with depth > 100
passConditions: T191 reads maxDepth from FREEDOM config xiigen.schema.dagMaxDepth
traversal exceeding maxDepth → DataProcessResult.failure('DEPTH_EXCEEDED')
depth counter checked per DFS iteration
resolution: T191 reads xiigen.schema.dagMaxDepth (default 100). Exceeding maxDepth → DataProcessResult.failure('DEPTH_EXCEEDED'). No limit = PERFORMANCE_VIOLATION.

description: T191 MUST handle empty DAG (0 nodes) without error, returning {hasCycle: false, cyclePath: []}.
blockConditions: T191 throws or returns failure on empty node list
T191 accesses nodes[0] without guard causing TypeError
empty DAG treated as invalid input
passConditions: T191 returns DataProcessResult.success({hasCycle: false, cyclePath: []}) for empty node list
no error thrown for 0-node DAG
resolution: T191 guard: if nodes.length === 0 → DataProcessResult.success({hasCycle: false, cyclePath: []}). Throwing on empty DAG = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: T191 MUST return the full cycle path when hasCycle=true. hasCycle=true without cyclePath = INCOMPLETE_OUTPUT.
blockConditions: T191 returns {hasCycle: true} without cyclePath array
cycle path omitted from result
DFS does not track path on GRAY node encounter
passConditions: T191 result includes cyclePath: string[] when hasCycle=true
cyclePath traces full cycle from entry node to repeated GRAY node
hasCycle=false returns cyclePath=[]
resolution: T191 result: {hasCycle: boolean, cyclePath: string[]}. hasCycle=true requires full cycle path. Missing path = INCOMPLETE_OUTPUT.

description: T192 MUST deduplicate edges before storing. Duplicate edge (same from+to) MUST be skipped with log warning.
blockConditions: T192 stores duplicate edges (same from+to stored twice)
T192 fails on duplicate edge instead of deduplicating
no deduplication check before storeDocument on edge
passConditions: T192 checks for existing edge {from, to} before storing
duplicate edge: skip storeDocument and log warning
idempotency key = schemaId + ':' + dependsOnSchemaId
resolution: T192 deduplicates: check existing {from, to} before store. Duplicate → skip + log. Storing duplicate edges = DATA_INTEGRITY_VIOLATION.

description: T192 MUST NOT cascade-delete edges on schema removal. Mark edges as {orphaned: true, orphanedAt} instead.
blockConditions: T192 deletes all edges pointing to a removed schema
T192 calls deleteDocument on edges during schema removal
cascade delete removes DAG edges referencing removed schema
passConditions: T192 sets {orphaned: true, orphanedAt: now()} on affected edges
db.deleteDocument never called by T192 for cascade
orphaned edges retained for audit trail
resolution: T192 on schema removal: storeDocument({orphaned: true, orphanedAt}) on edges. NEVER calls deleteDocument for cascade. Cascade delete = AUDIT_VIOLATION.

description: T193 MUST classify field type changes (int→string) as BREAKING even when field name is unchanged.
blockConditions: T193 classifies type change as ADDITIVE because field name is unchanged
T193 only checks additions/deletions, not type mutations
int→string reclassified as ADDITIVE routed to fast path
passConditions: T193 detects type change for any field and classifies as BREAKING
T193 checks: if existingField.type !== newField.type → BREAKING
resolution: T193: any field type change = BREAKING. Type changed but name unchanged treated as ADDITIVE = INCORRECT_CLASSIFICATION.

description: T193 MUST classify adding a required field as BREAKING. Existing documents lack the new required field.
blockConditions: T193 classifies required field addition as ADDITIVE
required field addition routed to SchemaQueued fast path
T193 does not distinguish optional vs required addition
passConditions: T193 checks field.required flag for newly added fields
required field addition → BREAKING
optional field addition → ADDITIVE
resolution: T193: new field with required=true = BREAKING (existing docs lack it). new optional field = ADDITIVE. Required addition as ADDITIVE = INCORRECT_CLASSIFICATION.

description: T193 MUST compare against the previously PUBLISHED (status=ACTIVE) version, NOT against latest DRAFT.
blockConditions: T193 compares against latest DRAFT version
T193 fetches schema by latest timestamp regardless of status
compatibility baseline is not the last ACTIVE published version
passConditions: T193 queries {schemaId, status: 'ACTIVE'} sorted by publishedAt DESC LIMIT 1
comparison baseline is always the last ACTIVE version
DRAFT versions ignored as baseline
resolution: T193 baseline: last ACTIVE version via {status:'ACTIVE', schemaId} sorted publishedAt DESC LIMIT 1. Using DRAFT = INCORRECT_BASELINE.

description: T193 MUST emit SchemaCompatibilityChecked event before returning. Missing event = AUDIT_VIOLATION.
blockConditions: T193 returns result without emitting SchemaCompatibilityChecked
T189 reads T193 result via direct function call
SchemaCompatibilityChecked event absent from T193
passConditions: T193 stores compatibility record then enqueues SchemaCompatibilityChecked
DNA-8: storeDocument before enqueue
event payload: {schemaId, isCompatible, changeType}
resolution: T193: storeDocument then enqueue SchemaCompatibilityChecked. Inline return without event = AUDIT_VIOLATION per DNA-8.

description: T194 MUST set status=ACTIVE and activeFrom=now() on publish. Schema with status=DRAFT after T194 = BUILD_FAILURE.
blockConditions: T194 stores schema with status=DRAFT after publish
activeFrom field absent from published schema
T194 does not update schema status during publish
passConditions: T194 storeDocumentWithOCC sets {status: 'ACTIVE', activeFrom: new Date().toISOString()}
schema record after T194 has status=ACTIVE
resolution: T194: storeDocumentWithOCC({status:'ACTIVE', activeFrom: now()}). Schema status=DRAFT after publish = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: T194 MUST NOT publish if a T202 approval record for this schema has status=PENDING.
blockConditions: T194 publishes schema while approval is PENDING
T194 does not check approval status before OCC publish
approval bypassed — T194 publishes regardless of T202 state
passConditions: T194 queries xiigen-schema-approvals for {schemaId, status: 'PENDING'} before publishing
PENDING found → DataProcessResult.failure('APPROVAL_PENDING')
T194 proceeds only when no PENDING approval exists
resolution: T194: check {schemaId, status:'PENDING'} in approvals before OCC. PENDING found → DataProcessResult.failure('APPROVAL_PENDING'). Bypass = GOVERNANCE_VIOLATION.

description: T195 MUST re-index in paginated batches. PAGE_SIZE from FREEDOM config xiigen.schema.indexPageSize (default 200). Single batch = PERFORMANCE_VIOLATION.
blockConditions: T195 loads all schemas at once without pagination
db.searchDocuments called without size parameter
re-index runs in single batch regardless of count
passConditions: T195 uses while loop with fromOffset += PAGE_SIZE
db.searchDocuments called with size=PAGE_SIZE per batch
loop exits when results.length < PAGE_SIZE
resolution: T195 re-index: while loop with fromOffset += PAGE_SIZE (default 200 from xiigen.schema.indexPageSize). Single batch = PERFORMANCE_VIOLATION.

description: T195 MUST skip schemas with status=DEPRECATED during re-index. Including deprecated schemas pollutes search results.
blockConditions: T195 re-indexes all schemas regardless of status
deprecated schemas included in re-index batch
filter {deprecated: false} absent from re-index searchDocuments
passConditions: T195 searchDocuments filter includes {deprecated: false}
no deprecated schema processed during re-index
resolution: T195 re-index filter: {deprecated: false}. Including deprecated schemas = INCORRECT_INDEX.

description: T195 MUST store run completion record in xiigen-schema-index-runs with startedAt, completedAt, processedCount. Missing = AUDIT_VIOLATION.
blockConditions: T195 stores no run record in xiigen-schema-index-runs
completedAt or processedCount absent from run record
passConditions: T195 stores {runId, startedAt, completedAt, processedCount, status:'COMPLETE'} after loop
storeDocument called for run record after completion
resolution: T195: storeDocument({runId, startedAt, completedAt, processedCount, status:'COMPLETE'}) after loop. Missing run record = AUDIT_VIOLATION.

description: T196 MUST return DataProcessResult.failure('SCHEMA_NOT_FOUND') if schema does not exist. NEVER return null.
blockConditions: T196 returns null when schema not found
caller must null-check T196 result instead of DataProcessResult
passConditions: T196 returns DataProcessResult.failure('SCHEMA_NOT_FOUND') when getDocumentWithVersion returns null
no null/undefined returned from T196
resolution: T196: null result from getDocumentWithVersion → DataProcessResult.failure('SCHEMA_NOT_FOUND'). Returning null = BUILD_FAILURE per DNA-3.

description: T196 MUST validate returned document's schemaId matches requested schemaId before returning versionPin.
blockConditions: T196 returns versionPin without verifying schemaId matches
T196 returns versionPin for wrong schema on document ID collision
passConditions: T196 checks result.schemaId === requestedSchemaId before returning
mismatch → DataProcessResult.failure('SCHEMA_MISMATCH')
resolution: T196: result.schemaId === requested schemaId guard. Mismatch → DataProcessResult.failure('SCHEMA_MISMATCH'). Missing guard = SECURITY_VIOLATION.

description: T196 MUST NOT call storeDocument or deleteDocument. Version reader is read-only.
blockConditions: T196 calls storeDocument for version cache update
T196 calls storeDocumentWithOCC during version read
passConditions: T196 only calls db.getDocumentWithVersion() and db.getDocument()
no write operations in T196
resolution: T196 is read-only: only getDocumentWithVersion/getDocument. Any storeDocument in T196 = ARCHITECTURE_VIOLATION.

description: T197 MUST reject topology build if T191 reports a cycle. Return DataProcessResult.failure('CYCLE_DETECTED') immediately.
blockConditions: T197 builds partial topology when cycle detected
T197 ignores T191 cycle detection result
T197 does not call T191 before building topology
passConditions: T197 calls T191 DagCycleDetectorService before building
hasCycle=true → DataProcessResult.failure('CYCLE_DETECTED')
topology only built when T191 returns {hasCycle: false}
resolution: T197: call T191 first. hasCycle=true → DataProcessResult.failure('CYCLE_DETECTED'). Partial topology on cycle = DATA_INTEGRITY_VIOLATION.

description: T197 topology output MUST include BFS depth level for each node. Adjacency-list-only without levels = INCOMPLETE_OUTPUT.
blockConditions: T197 output is adjacency list only without level per node
BFS not performed — topological sort only
level field absent from topology node objects
passConditions: T197 performs BFS from root nodes to assign level to each node
each topology node includes {nodeId, level, edges: []}
root nodes have level=0
resolution: T197 per-node output: {nodeId, level: number, edges: string[]}. BFS from roots assigns level. Missing level = INCOMPLETE_OUTPUT.

description: T197 MUST paginate node fetch from xiigen-schema-dag in batches. Loading all nodes at once = PERFORMANCE_VIOLATION.
blockConditions: T197 loads all DAG nodes in single searchDocuments call
db.searchDocuments called without size parameter
pagination loop absent from T197 node fetch
passConditions: T197 uses while loop with fromOffset += PAGE_SIZE
db.searchDocuments called with size=PAGE_SIZE per batch
loop exits when results.length < PAGE_SIZE
resolution: T197 node fetch: while loop with fromOffset += PAGE_SIZE. Single batch load = PERFORMANCE_VIOLATION.

description: T197 MUST deduplicate edges in topology output. Duplicate edges in output = DATA_INTEGRITY_VIOLATION.
blockConditions: T197 output includes duplicate edges (same {from, to} pair twice)
T197 does not deduplicate edges from xiigen-schema-dag
edge deduplication absent from topology build
passConditions: T197 uses Set keyed by 'from:to' to deduplicate edges
topology output adjacency list contains no duplicate edges
resolution: T197 deduplicates edges via Set(edgeKey = from+':'+to). Duplicate edges in output = DATA_INTEGRITY_VIOLATION.

description: T198 MUST reject empty-query + empty-filter requests. DataProcessResult.failure('QUERY_REQUIRED') returned.
blockConditions: T198 accepts empty query with no filters and returns all schemas
T198 does not validate at least one search criterion
full-table scan possible via T198
passConditions: T198 checks: if !query && Object.keys(filters).length === 0 → DataProcessResult.failure('QUERY_REQUIRED')
at least one criterion required
resolution: T198: if !query AND no filters → DataProcessResult.failure('QUERY_REQUIRED'). Accepting empty search = PERFORMANCE_VIOLATION.

description: T198 MUST paginate search results. Returning unlimited results = PERFORMANCE_VIOLATION.
blockConditions: T198 returns all matching schemas without pagination
db.searchDocuments called without size/from parameters
passConditions: T198 accepts page and pageSize (defaults: page=0, pageSize=20 from FREEDOM config)
db.searchDocuments called with {size: pageSize, from: page * pageSize}
returns {items, total, page, pageSize}
resolution: T198: db.searchDocuments({size: pageSize, from: offset}). Return {items, total, page, pageSize}. Unlimited return = PERFORMANCE_VIOLATION.

description: T198 MUST exclude deprecated schemas by default. includeDeprecated=true flag required to include them.
blockConditions: T198 includes deprecated schemas in results by default
deprecated=true filter absent unless explicitly requested
passConditions: T198 default filter includes {deprecated: false}
includeDeprecated=true flag removes the deprecated:false filter
resolution: T198 default: {deprecated: false} in filter. includeDeprecated=true omits this. Deprecated included by default = INCORRECT_SEARCH_RESULTS.

description: T198 result documents MUST include schema.version and schema.status. Omitting these = INCOMPLETE_OUTPUT.
blockConditions: T198 returns documents without version field
T198 projects only schemaId and name, omitting version and status
passConditions: T198 result documents include at minimum: schemaId, name, version, status, namespace
version and status present in all search result items
resolution: T198 result fields: {schemaId, name, version, status, namespace, ...}. Missing version or status = INCOMPLETE_OUTPUT.

description: T199 MUST return placeholder Mermaid for 0-node DAG. NEVER return null or empty string.
blockConditions: T199 returns null for empty DAG
T199 returns empty string for 0-node DAG
T199 passes empty node list to renderer without guard
passConditions: T199 returns 'graph TD\n  empty["No schemas registered"]' for 0-node DAG
DataProcessResult.success with placeholder string for empty DAG
resolution: T199: 0 nodes → DataProcessResult.success('graph TD\n  empty["No schemas registered"]'). Null or empty string = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: T199 MUST limit render to MAX_NODES from FREEDOM config xiigen.schema.renderMaxNodes (default 500). Oversized DAG = DAG_TOO_LARGE.
blockConditions: T199 renders DAGs with more than 500 nodes without limit
MAX_NODES not read from FREEDOM config
T199 passes all nodes to renderer regardless of count
passConditions: T199 reads maxNodes from xiigen.schema.renderMaxNodes (default 500)
nodes.length > maxNodes → DataProcessResult.failure('DAG_TOO_LARGE')
node count checked before delegation to dagRendererHandler
resolution: T199 reads xiigen.schema.renderMaxNodes (default 500). nodes > maxNodes → DataProcessResult.failure('DAG_TOO_LARGE'). No limit = PERFORMANCE_VIOLATION.

description: T199 MUST include schema version labels on rendered nodes (e.g. T189["SchemaName v1.2.0"]). Missing version = INCOMPLETE_OUTPUT.
blockConditions: T199 renders nodes with schemaId only, no version label
Mermaid node labels omit version
dagRendererHandler called without version data
passConditions: T199 passes {nodeId, name, version} per node to dagRendererHandler
Mermaid format: nodeId["name v{version}"]
version included in each rendered node label
resolution: T199 node label: nodeId["schemaName v{version}"]. Node without version = INCOMPLETE_OUTPUT.

description: T200 MUST check for active dependents before deprecating. Active dependents → emit SchemaDeprecationBlocked.
blockConditions: T200 deprecates without checking active dependents
T200 does not query xiigen-schema-dag for edges pointing to this schema
SchemaDeprecationBlocked event never emitted
passConditions: T200 queries xiigen-schema-dag for {to: schemaId, orphaned: false}
dependents found → storeDocument({status:'DEPRECATION_BLOCKED'}) + emit SchemaDeprecationBlocked
no dependents → proceed with deprecated=true
resolution: T200 checks active dependents {to: schemaId, orphaned: false}. Found → emit SchemaDeprecationBlocked. Skipping = DATA_INTEGRITY_VIOLATION.

description: T200 deprecation TTL MUST come from FREEDOM config xiigen.schema.deprecationTtlDays (default 90). Hardcoded TTL = Config_VIOLATION.
blockConditions: T200 uses hardcoded TTL without reading FREEDOM config
deprecationTtlDays absent from FREEDOM key reads
deprecation window fixed in source code
passConditions: T200 reads xiigen.schema.deprecationTtlDays from FREEDOM config
deprecatedAt + ttlDays → removalEligibleAt
FREEDOM absence falls back to 90
resolution: T200 reads xiigen.schema.deprecationTtlDays (default 90). Hardcoded TTL = Config_VIOLATION per Rule 14.

description: T200 MUST emit SchemaDeprecated event after successful soft-delete. Missing event = AUDIT_VIOLATION.
blockConditions: T200 soft-deletes without emitting SchemaDeprecated
T200 stores deprecated record but does not enqueue event
passConditions: T200 enqueues SchemaDeprecated after storeDocument per DNA-8
payload: {schemaId, deprecatedAt, tenantId}
resolution: T200: storeDocument({deprecated:true, deprecatedAt}) then enqueue SchemaDeprecated. Missing event = AUDIT_VIOLATION per DNA-8.

description: T201 MUST store full schema snapshot at publish time — NOT only diff. Diff-only prevents point-in-time reconstruction.
blockConditions: T201 stores only changed fields (diff) in history record
history record contains delta only
point-in-time reconstruction not possible from history
passConditions: T201 stores complete schema body in history record at SchemaPublished event
each history record is self-contained for point-in-time reconstruction
resolution: T201: full schema snapshot per publish. Diff-only history = COMPLIANCE_VIOLATION (reconstruction broken).

description: T201 history records MUST include actor={userId, tenantId} from ALS context. Missing actor = AUDIT_VIOLATION.
blockConditions: T201 stores history record without actor field
userId absent from history record
actor hardcoded or null
passConditions: T201 reads userId from ClsService ALS context
history record includes {actor: {userId, tenantId}}
resolution: T201 history: actor: {userId: cls.get().userId, tenantId: cls.get().tenantId} required. Missing = AUDIT_VIOLATION.

description: T201 MUST NOT delete or update history records on schema deprecation or deletion. History is immutable.
blockConditions: T201 deletes history records when schema is deprecated
T201 updates existing history records on schema change
history removed on schema deletion
passConditions: T201 never calls deleteDocument or storeDocumentWithOCC on xiigen-schema-history
history index is append-only
resolution: T201: no deleteDocument or storeDocumentWithOCC on history ever. Deleted on deprecation = COMPLIANCE_VIOLATION.

description: T202 DEFER TTL MUST come from FREEDOM config xiigen.schema.approvalDeferTtlHours (default 72). Hardcoded TTL = Config_VIOLATION.
blockConditions: T202 uses hardcoded DEFER TTL
approvalDeferTtlHours not read from FREEDOM config
passConditions: T202 reads xiigen.schema.approvalDeferTtlHours (default 72)
deferredUntil = now() + ttlHours in SchemaApprovalDeferred payload
resolution: T202 reads xiigen.schema.approvalDeferTtlHours (default 72h). Hardcoded TTL = Config_VIOLATION per Rule 14.

description: T202 MUST prevent double-approval. Re-approving status=APPROVED schema = DataProcessResult.failure('ALREADY_APPROVED').
blockConditions: T202 processes APPROVE even when already APPROVED
no idempotency check before APPROVE action
double-approval overwrites prior record
passConditions: T202 checks for {schemaId, status:'APPROVED'} before processing
found → DataProcessResult.failure('ALREADY_APPROVED')
resolution: T202: check existing {schemaId, status:'APPROVED'} before APPROVE. Found → DataProcessResult.failure('ALREADY_APPROVED'). Double approval = IDEMPOTENCY_VIOLATION.

description: T202 MUST storeDocument BEFORE emitting approval event for all three outcomes. DNA-8.
blockConditions: T202 enqueues approval event before storing record
SchemaApprovalGranted emitted before storeDocument
passConditions: T202 calls storeDocument before enqueue for APPROVED/REJECTED/DEFERRED
DNA-8 order enforced for all three approval outcomes
resolution: T202: storeDocument BEFORE enqueue for all outcomes. Enqueue before store = BUILD_FAILURE per DNA-8.

description: T203 MUST support dryRun=true mode. In dry run, no documents modified, affected count returned.
blockConditions: T203 does not support dryRun flag
T203 applies migration regardless of dryRun value
passConditions: T203 accepts dryRun: boolean parameter
dryRun=true: count matching documents without storeDocument
result includes {processedCount, dryRun: boolean}
resolution: T203 dryRun=true: count records, no storeDocument. dryRun=false: migrate. Missing dryRun = ARCHITECTURE_VIOLATION.

description: T203 MUST store run record with status=IN_PROGRESS BEFORE processing any documents. DNA-8.
blockConditions: T203 stores migration run record after processing completes
run record absent until all batches complete
passConditions: T203 stores {runId, startedAt, status:'IN_PROGRESS'} BEFORE first batch
runId available for status updates throughout migration
resolution: T203: storeDocument({runId, status:'IN_PROGRESS'}) BEFORE first batch. Record after completion = DNA-8_VIOLATION.

description: T203 MUST set run status=FAILED and update record if any batch fails. Partial migration without status update = AUDIT_VIOLATION.
blockConditions: T203 does not update run record when batch fails
run record remains IN_PROGRESS on error
passConditions: T203 wraps batch loop in try/catch
catch: storeDocumentWithOCC({status:'FAILED', failedAt, error})
finally: storeDocumentWithOCC({status:'COMPLETE', completedAt}) on success
resolution: T203 try/catch: failure → storeDocumentWithOCC({status:'FAILED', failedAt}). No status update on failure = AUDIT_VIOLATION.

description: T204 MUST detect NAMESPACE_COLLISION: two ACTIVE schemas with same name in same namespace.
blockConditions: T204 does not check for duplicate schema names within namespace
NAMESPACE_COLLISION type not implemented
passConditions: T204 queries {namespace, name, status:'ACTIVE'} and checks count > 1
count > 1 → NAMESPACE_COLLISION conflict
result includes all conflicting schema IDs
resolution: T204: searchDocuments({namespace, name, status:'ACTIVE'}). count > 1 → NAMESPACE_COLLISION. Missing check = DATA_INTEGRITY_VIOLATION.

description: T204 MUST NOT resolve conflicts — detection and reporting only. Auto-resolution = ARCHITECTURE_VIOLATION.
blockConditions: T204 auto-resolves by deprecating older schema
T204 calls storeDocument to resolve conflicts inline
passConditions: T204 only reads (searchDocuments, getDocument) — no writes
proposedResolution=null in all T204 results
resolution: T204 is detection-only: no storeDocument, no deleteDocument. proposedResolution=null always. Auto-resolution = ARCHITECTURE_VIOLATION.

description: T204 MUST emit SchemaConflictDetected with {conflictType, conflictingSchemas, proposedResolution: null}. Missing = AUDIT_VIOLATION.
blockConditions: T204 returns conflict without emitting event
SchemaConflictDetected absent from T204 output
passConditions: T204 stores conflict record then enqueues SchemaConflictDetected
DNA-8: storeDocument before enqueue
payload: {conflictType, conflictingSchemas: string[], proposedResolution: null}
resolution: T204: storeDocument(conflict) then enqueue SchemaConflictDetected. proposedResolution=null always. Missing event = AUDIT_VIOLATION per DNA-8.

description: T204 MUST detect VERSION_CONFLICT: two ACTIVE versions of same schema in same namespace.
blockConditions: T204 allows multiple ACTIVE versions to coexist
VERSION_CONFLICT detection absent
T204 only checks NAMESPACE_COLLISION
passConditions: T204 queries {schemaId, status:'ACTIVE'} and checks count > 1
count > 1 → VERSION_CONFLICT
both NAMESPACE_COLLISION and VERSION_CONFLICT checks run in T204
resolution: T204: searchDocuments({schemaId, status:'ACTIVE'}). count > 1 → VERSION_CONFLICT. Missing = DATA_INTEGRITY_VIOLATION.

description: T205 MUST return ALL validation errors in single result. Fail-fast (stop at first error) = INCORRECT_VALIDATION.
blockConditions: T205 stops validation on first error found
T205 returns only first validation error
fail-fast used in T205
passConditions: T205 collects all validation errors before returning
result includes errors: ValidationError[] with all failures
resolution: T205: collect all errors, return complete list. Fail-fast = INCORRECT_VALIDATION.

description: T205 MUST NOT trigger T190 or perform any write. Validation is read-only and side-effect-free.
blockConditions: T205 calls T190 SchemaVersionManager during validation
T205 stores validation result before returning
T205 enqueues events as part of validation
passConditions: T205 only calls db.searchDocuments and db.getDocument
no enqueue, no storeDocument in T205
T190 not called from T205
resolution: T205 pure read: getDocument/searchDocuments only. No storeDocument, no enqueue, no T190. Any write = ARCHITECTURE_VIOLATION.

description: T205 MUST emit SchemaValidationFailed when validation fails. Missing event on failure = AUDIT_VIOLATION.
blockConditions: T205 returns failure without emitting SchemaValidationFailed
T205 stores no record on validation failure
passConditions: T205 stores validation record then enqueues SchemaValidationFailed on failure
DNA-8: storeDocument before enqueue
payload: {schemaId, errors: ValidationError[]}
resolution: T205: failure → storeDocument(record) then enqueue SchemaValidationFailed. Missing event = AUDIT_VIOLATION per DNA-8.

description: T206 MUST produce quality scores in [0.0, 1.0]. Score outside range = BUILD_FAILURE.
blockConditions: T206 produces score outside 0.0–1.0
quality score not clamped
passConditions: T206 clamps: Math.min(1.0, Math.max(0.0, rawScore))
DataProcessResult includes score in [0.0, 1.0]
resolution: T206: score = Math.min(1.0, Math.max(0.0, rawScore)). Score outside [0.0, 1.0] = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: T206 MUST store quality scores in xiigen-schema-quality — NOT inline in schema record. Inline modification = ARCHITECTURE_VIOLATION.
blockConditions: T206 calls storeDocumentWithOCC to add qualityScore to schema record
T206 modifies xiigen-schemas index
passConditions: T206 stores {schemaId, qualityScore, analyzedAt} in xiigen-schema-quality
T206 never writes to xiigen-schemas
resolution: T206: xiigen-schema-quality only. Never writes to xiigen-schemas. Modifying schema record = ARCHITECTURE_VIOLATION.

description: T206 cron schedule MUST come from FREEDOM config xiigen.schema.qualityAnalysisCron (default '0 2 * * *'). Hardcoded cron = Config_VIOLATION.
blockConditions: T206 uses hardcoded @Cron('0 2 * * *')
qualityAnalysisCron not read from FREEDOM config
passConditions: T206 reads xiigen.schema.qualityAnalysisCron from FREEDOM config
FREEDOM absence falls back to '0 2 * * *'
resolution: T206 reads xiigen.schema.qualityAnalysisCron (default '0 2 * * *'). Hardcoded @Cron = Config_VIOLATION per Rule 14.

description: T207 MUST chunk exports. CHUNK_SIZE from FREEDOM config xiigen.schema.exportChunkSize (default 1000). Single batch = PERFORMANCE_VIOLATION.
blockConditions: T207 exports all schemas in single batch
CHUNK_SIZE not read from FREEDOM config
passConditions: T207 reads xiigen.schema.exportChunkSize (default 1000)
while loop with offset += chunkSize
each chunk stored as separate batch record
resolution: T207: while loop with offset += chunkSize (default 1000). Single batch = PERFORMANCE_VIOLATION.

description: T207 MUST include export metadata as first record: {tenantId, exportedAt, schemaCount, format}. Missing = INCOMPLETE_OUTPUT.
blockConditions: T207 export omits metadata header
tenantId absent from export metadata
passConditions: T207 first record: {tenantId: cls.get().tenantId, exportedAt, schemaCount, format}
metadata stored in xiigen-schema-exports before schema records
resolution: T207: first export record = {tenantId, exportedAt, schemaCount, format}. Missing header = INCOMPLETE_OUTPUT.

description: T207 MUST store export audit record in xiigen-schema-exports BEFORE streaming schema data. DNA-8.
blockConditions: T207 stores audit record after export completes
DNA-8 violated: data streamed before audit record stored
passConditions: T207 stores {exportId, tenantId, startedAt, status:'IN_PROGRESS'} before first chunk
status updated to 'COMPLETE' after all chunks
resolution: T207: storeDocument(exportAudit {status:'IN_PROGRESS'}) BEFORE first chunk. Audit after streaming = DNA-8_VIOLATION.

description: T208 MUST support format=dot as third valid format. Unknown formats not in {mermaid, json, dot} = ROUTE_REJECTED.
blockConditions: T208 only handles mermaid and json
format=dot returns error or ROUTE_REJECTED
passConditions: T208 routes format=dot to DotRenderer
format=mermaid → T199, format=json → T197, format=dot → DotRenderer
unknown format → DataProcessResult.failure('ROUTE_REJECTED')
resolution: T208 valid: {mermaid, json, dot}. dot → DotRenderer. Unknown → DataProcessResult.failure('ROUTE_REJECTED'). Missing dot = INCOMPLETE_ROUTING.

description: T208 MUST return cached visualization on DAG hash match. Re-rendering unchanged DAG = PERFORMANCE_VIOLATION.
blockConditions: T208 re-renders on every request without cache check
content-addressed DAG cache absent
passConditions: T208 computes dagHash before rendering
cache hit (same dagHash) → return cached visualization
cache miss → render + store with dagHash
resolution: T208: dagHash check first. hit → cached result. miss → render + cache. No cache = PERFORMANCE_VIOLATION.

description: T208 MUST return {status: 'EMPTY_DAG', visualization: null} for 0-node DAG. NEVER empty string or null.
blockConditions: T208 returns empty string for 0-node DAG
T208 returns null visualization for empty DAG
passConditions: T208 checks node count before delegation
nodes.length === 0 → DataProcessResult.success({status:'EMPTY_DAG', visualization: null})
resolution: T208: 0 nodes → {status:'EMPTY_DAG', visualization: null}. Empty string = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: T207 MUST validate export format parameter. Supported formats: {json, csv, ndjson}. Unknown format = DataProcessResult.failure("INVALID_EXPORT_FORMAT").
blockConditions: T207 accepts unknown export format without error
format validation absent from T207
unsupported format silently produces empty output
passConditions: T207 checks format against allowed set {json, csv, ndjson}
unknown format → DataProcessResult.failure("INVALID_EXPORT_FORMAT")
format validation runs before any data fetch
resolution: T207 validates format in {json, csv, ndjson} before fetch. Unknown format → DataProcessResult.failure("INVALID_EXPORT_FORMAT"). Silent unknown = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: T189 MUST be idempotent — re-submitting same schemaId+version MUST return existing record, NOT create duplicate.
blockConditions: T189 creates duplicate schema records for same schemaId+version
T189 does not check for existing schema before registering
duplicate schema registration causes multiple ACTIVE records
passConditions: T189 queries {schemaId, version} before registering
existing record found → return DataProcessResult.success(existingRecord) without re-registering
no duplicate schema records in xiigen-schemas
resolution: T189: check {schemaId, version} before store. Existing → return success(existing). No check = DATA_INTEGRITY_VIOLATION.

description: T202 MUST auto-reject DEFERRED approvals past their TTL. Scheduler checks for expired DEFERRED records and emits SchemaApprovalExpired.
blockConditions: T202 deferred approvals never auto-expire
expired deferred approvals remain status=DEFERRED indefinitely
no scheduled TTL expiry check for deferred approvals
passConditions: T202 scheduled job queries {status:"DEFERRED", deferredUntil: {lt: now()}}
expired records → storeDocumentWithOCC({status:"EXPIRED"}) + emit SchemaApprovalExpired
DNA-8: store before enqueue for expiry
resolution: T202 scheduler: find {status:"DEFERRED", deferredUntil < now()} → storeDocumentWithOCC({status:"EXPIRED"}) + enqueue SchemaApprovalExpired. No expiry = GOVERNANCE_VIOLATION.

description: T189 SchemaRegistrationHandler: xiigen-schemas index is PRIVATE scope. Cross-tenant schema reads are forbidden. Tenant context from AsyncLocalStorage only.
blockConditions: schema record stored with knowledgeScope:GLOBAL
tenantId absent from stored schema record
tenantId sourced from event payload instead of ALS
resolution: scope_isolation: { modelToken: 'AI_SCOPE_ARBITER', blind: true, blockSemanticsBehavior: 'ANY_BLOCK_CLASS_REJECTS' }
passConditions: Schema records stored with knowledgeScope: 'PRIVATE' and tenantId from AsyncLocalStorage
tenantId sourced exclusively from cls.get().tenantId — never from event payload
All xiigen-schemas reads for T189 include tenantId predicate from ALS

description: T190 SchemaChangeClassifier: operates on the schema submitted by the current tenant only. Classification results scoped to tenant context from ALS.
blockConditions: changeClassification record stored with knowledgeScope:GLOBAL
tenantId absent from classification record
tenantId sourced from event payload instead of ALS
resolution: scope_isolation: { modelToken: 'AI_SCOPE_ARBITER', blind: true, blockSemanticsBehavior: 'ANY_BLOCK_CLASS_REJECTS' }
passConditions: Change classification records stored with knowledgeScope: 'PRIVATE' and tenantId from AsyncLocalStorage
tenantId sourced exclusively from cls.get().tenantId — never from event payload

passConditions: DAG cycle detection operates exclusively on current tenant's schema DAG
tenantId predicate from ALS included in all DAG node lookup queries

passConditions: DAG edge records stored with knowledgeScope: 'PRIVATE' and tenantId from AsyncLocalStorage

passConditions: Audit records stored with knowledgeScope: 'PRIVATE' and tenantId from AsyncLocalStorage
tenantId sourced exclusively from cls.get().tenantId — never from event payload

description: T194 SchemaPublisher: xiigen-schemas index is PRIVATE scope. OCC publish must include tenantId in predicate.
blockConditions: schema record stored with knowledgeScope:GLOBAL
tenantId absent from storeDocumentWithOCC call
tenantId sourced from event payload instead of ALS
resolution: scope_isolation: { modelToken: 'AI_SCOPE_ARBITER', blind: true, blockSemanticsBehavior: 'ANY_BLOCK_CLASS_REJECTS' }
passConditions: Schema records stored with knowledgeScope: 'PRIVATE'; tenantId included in storeDocumentWithOCC call
tenantId sourced exclusively from cls.get().tenantId — never from event payload

description: T195 SchemaIndexManagerService: index management scoped to current tenant. Cross-tenant index management is forbidden.
blockConditions: tenant index created with cross-tenant access
index management bypasses tenant scope
tenantId absent from index management operations

passConditions: Index management operations scoped to current tenant — no cross-tenant index access
tenantId present in all index management operations in T195

description: T207 SchemaExportService: export scoped to current tenant's active schemas only. Cross-tenant schema export is a security violation.
blockConditions: schema export includes records from other tenants
tenantId absent from export searchDocuments predicate
export includes deprecated schemas (activeUntil != null)
